notes: Synthesized from adaptor surface; internal OpenFn Lightning collections key-value API. The adaptor's ast.json is empty and there is no published OpenAPI spec for Lightning collections, so endpoints/response shapes were reverse-engineered from the adaptor source (get/set/remove/each in collections.js) and its bundled mock Lightning server (mock.js). Confirmed shapes: GET /{name}/{key} -> {key,value} (204 if missing); GET /{name}?key&limit&cursor -> {items:[{key,value}],cursor}; POST /{name} {items:[{key,value}]} -> {upserted,errors}; DELETE /{name} or /{name}/{key} -> {deleted,keys}. Values are stored as strings (typically JSON-encoded). Auth is a Personal Access Token via Authorization: Bearer (collections_token); base path is the Lightning /collections endpoint (WORKER_COLLECTIONS_URL). Caveats: listCollections (GET /) and setCollectionItem (PUT /{name}/{key}) are synthesized conveniences not present verbatim in the adaptor; createdBefore/createdAfter query filters come from the adaptor QueryOptions typedef but are only partially honored by the reference mock; exact production Lightning response envelopes may differ.
